items.mistake: Running k-NN with Euclidean distance on raw mixed-scale features
items.fix: Fit a scaler (StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler) in a pipeline before `KNeighborsClassifier`, then cross-validate metric and k.
items.why: Large-magnitude columns dominate Minkowski sums; neighbors reflect units, not semantics.
items.example: Height in cm (~170) and income in dollars (~80,000) make every neighbor match on income; after z-scoring, age and income both influence the k=5 vote.

items.mistake: Using cosine similarity on vectors that were never normalized when magnitude matters
items.fix: Confirm whether scale is informative; if so, use Euclidean on scaled features or normalize only when direction is the signal.
items.why: Cosine discards length; two documents with very different term counts can look identical if proportions match.
items.example: Bag-of-words (1, 0, 1) and (100, 0, 100) have cosine 1.0 but very different L2 norms—the shorter doc may be noise, not a true duplicate.

items.mistake: Applying Manhattan distance in standard k-means without changing the algorithm
items.fix: Use k-means with Euclidean on scaled data, or switch to k-medians / algorithms designed for L1 geometry.
items.why: k-means centroids are arithmetic means that minimize sum of squared Euclidean distances; L1 is inconsistent with the update rule.
items.example: Replacing `metric='manhattan'` in k-NN is valid, but `KMeans` in sklearn always uses Euclidean—use `KMedoids` for L1-style clustering.
